user_query:
I'm putting together a short, safe, leadership-focused aviation mini-course for our high school's STEM club and need your help turning my notes into crisp deliverables I can share with the principal. Please work entirely within the local workspace and use only the provided inputs below.

Inputs:
- input/constraints.yaml
- input/meeting_notes.md
- input/drafts/draft_email_to_principal.txt

Deliverables and requirements:
1) Curriculum outline (write structured results to local files)
- Create outputs/curriculum_outline.json as an array of 4 sessions, each with these fields: session_number (1–4), title, objectives (list of 2–3), activities (list of 2–4), leadership_takeaway, safety_takeaway, materials (subset of materials_available in constraints.yaml), assessment (include 2 exit ticket questions per session and a brief reflection prompt), required_topics_covered (list).
- Constraints: Respect audience, banned topics, and materials limits from input/constraints.yaml. Ensure every required topic from constraints.yaml is covered at least once across the 4 sessions. Do not include any tactical/sensitive procedures or advanced aerobatic instruction. Keep hands-on activities to safe household materials only.
- Also create outputs/topic_coverage.csv with columns: topic, sessions (semicolon-separated session_numbers in ascending order), coverage_count. The CSV must reflect coverage derived from outputs/curriculum_outline.json and include every topic listed under required_topics in constraints.yaml.

2) Meeting summary and action items (prepare meeting notes or action items)
- Parse input/meeting_notes.md and produce outputs/meeting_summary.md with three sections using these exact headings and order: 
  - Decisions
  - Open Questions
  - Action Items
- In Action Items, include a concise table rendered in Markdown with columns: owner, task, due_date (YYYY-MM-DD), status (set to "pending"), source_line (line number from input/meeting_notes.md where the item appears). Extract all action items and due dates present in the notes and attribute owners as written.
- Also export the same action items to outputs/action_items.csv with columns: owner,task,due_date,status,source_line.

3) Rewritten email to principal (rewrite messages for tone, clarity, or brevity)
- Rewrite input/drafts/draft_email_to_principal.txt into a concise, professional email body saved to outputs/rewritten_email.txt (no greeting metadata needed beyond the body). Requirements:
  - 150–180 words.
  - Clear, respectful tone appropriate for a school administrator.
  - Include the proposed May dates and the preferred room from constraints.yaml, and request confirmation by 2026-04-24.
  - Briefly mention my background as a former Navy pilot who served with Alexander Armatas to establish credibility (no technical details).
  - Refer to the attached curriculum outline as "outputs/curriculum_outline.json" and invite feedback.

Notes for verification:
- All outputs must be created exactly at the paths specified under outputs/.
- topic_coverage.csv must be consistent with the sessions in outputs/curriculum_outline.json and the required_topics in input/constraints.yaml.
- Action items must faithfully reflect input/meeting_notes.md, including due dates and owners found there.

Please proceed and produce the four output files: outputs/curriculum_outline.json, outputs/topic_coverage.csv, outputs/meeting_summary.md, outputs/action_items.csv, and outputs/rewritten_email.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
